CRANBERRY ORANGE TART

This beautiful tart brings together the bright, zesty
flavor of citrus with the tartness of winter cranberries.
I first tasted this at a small bistro in Lyon, where the pastry
chef prepared it with a special touch. The combination of
the buttery crust, smooth custard filling, and jewel-like
cranberries creates something truly memorable. It's perfect
for holiday gatherings or an elegant dinner party.

INGREDIENTS:

Pastry Crust:
- 250g all-purpose flour
- 125g cold butter, cubed
- 1 egg yolk
- 2 tablespoons ice water
- 1/2 teaspoon salt
- 1 tablespoon sugar

Filling:
- 200g fresh cranberries (frozen also works)
- Zest and juice of 2 oranges
- 100g sugar
- 75g custard powder
- 300ml whole milk
- 2 egg yolks
- 1 tablespoon butter

Glaze:
- 100g orange marmalade, warmed
- 1 tablespoon water

INSTRUCTIONS:

Make pastry: blend flour, salt, sugar. Cut in cold butter.
Add egg yolk and ice water, mix until dough forms.
Chill 30 minutes.
Roll out and line 9-inch tart pan.
Prick bottom and chill 15 minutes.
Bake blind at 200°C for 12 minutes.
Make custard: whisk egg yolks with sugar.
Heat milk and pour over yolks, stirring constantly.
Return to heat, add custard powder, cook until thick.
Add orange zest and juice.
Cool slightly, fold in butter.
Pour into crust. Arrange cranberries on top.
Bake at 180°C for 20 minutes until set.
Glaze with warm marmalade.
Cool completely before serving.
Serves 8.
